sources.table_caption: TABLE I. Quantitative results of clutter removal. We report mean and standard deviation of grasp success rates (GSR) and declutter rates (DR). Edge-sample randomly sample edges that do not collide with the table. GIGA-High query at a higher resolution of 60 × 60 × 60 .

TABLE I: Clutter removal performance under single-view, fixed camera pose, and gamma noise conditions. We report the mean and standard deviation of GSR and DR. 𝑁 denotes sampling rounds in IGD. '*' denotes the results are from [2] since we use the same experiment settings. The latency is evaluated on RTX 4070 GPU except for OrbitGrasp, because OrbitGrasp requires over 40GB of GPU memory. Thus, we test OrbitGrasp on RTX A6000. The best performances are highlighted in blue , and the second-best performances are highlighted in pink .

TABLE II: Clutter removal performance under single-view, random camera pose, and Gaussian noise conditions. The results except EquiGIGA and EquiIGD are from [20] and [6] since we use the same experiment settings. We report the mean and standard deviation of GSR and DR. The best performances are highlighted in blue , and the second-best performances are highlighted in pink .

TABLE III: Quantitative results of clutter removal in the real-world experiment. We report GSR, DR, successful grasp numbers, and total grasp trial numbers (in brackets). The best performances are highlighted in blue , and the second-best performances are highlighted in pink .
